items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list

children.content: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

image.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track

items.title: Woman charged with murder following investigation into death of 24yo

children.content: The woman, 48, and the deceased were known to each other, according to Homicide Squad detectives.

image.altText: A shot of a grey building with the words 'central law courts' written on the side.

items.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes

children.content: The long weekend is off to a tragic start after two motorcyclists die in separate crashes near Perth, while a woman, 18, is killed in a four-wheel drive rollover in WA's north.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Does a 'historic' plan to protect WA's largest river go far enough?

children.content: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

image.altText: A river with treed land on either side.

items.title: Hospitality operators call for end to Rottnest's staffing advantage

children.content: Visa rules allow backpackers to conduct "remote work" on the island, just 33km from Perth's city centre, but not in WA's South West — something business owners want to change.

gallery.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

thumbnail.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

items.title: 'Could get worse': WA Easter holiday-makers anxious amid fuel crisis

children.content: Accommodation providers in regional WA tourism hotspots see a high number of cancellations, as holiday-makers choose to stay close to home due to fuel prices.

image.altText: Large waves crash onto a rocky headland.

items.title: Dockers claim thriller after Crow hits post inside final minute

children.content: Fremantle coughs up a five-goal lead before holding on in the final quarter after Adelaide's James Borlase hits the post with 30 seconds left on the clock.

image.altText: AFL players in purple and white high five

items.title: Why local fish may be hard to find this Easter in WA

children.content: This Easter is the first in Western Australia under tough new demersal fishing rules. Seafood retailers say the change in supply and price is noticeable.

image.altText: a person purchasing fish from a van

items.title: Easter temperatures to drop in Perth as another Queensland cyclone looms

children.content: For most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter — but Western Australia perhaps has the most interesting weather ahead.

image.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

items.title: Police plunge into Perth's Swan River to rescue woman

children.content: Two police officers who swam out into Perth's Swan River early on Thursday to help rescue a woman will be recommended for a bravery award.

image.altText: A woman clings to a buoy in the Swan River as a police officer swims up to her.

items.title: How do you clean a toilet brush and when should you change it?

children.content: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

items.title: As more Australian businesses expand into Asia, here's some cultural differences to consider

items.title: How Australia's leaders have changed their language on the Iran war

children.content: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, world oil markets rattled and no obvious off-ramp to the hostilities in sight, the major parties have dialled back their initial optimism.

items.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences wrote of Bondi attacks in diary, court told

children.content: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice, which are making their way into residential areas.

items.title: States and territories agree to extra fuel excise cut using GST revenue

children.content: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

articles.title: WA invokes emergency powers to address fuel supply crisis

children.content: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

articles.title: Perth's 'Cheap fuel Tuesdays' are off the table. Here's why

children.content: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.
